relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The judging human is caught in the same mirror.
Divine judgment meets reality according to truth.
Judgment of others cannot become escape from judgment.
Kindness, restraint, and patience lead toward turning.
The unturned heart stores up the unveiling of justice.
Each receives according to works.
Enduring good seeks glory, honor, incorruptibility, and age-long life.
Selfish ambition obeys injustice and meets wrath.
Harm brings pressure upon every human soul.
Good receives glory, honor, and peace without ethnic favoritism.
The Divine shows no partiality.
Law-possession and Law-absence do not bypass accountability.
Hearing Law is not the same as doing it.
Nations without Torah can still embody Torah-work.
The heart can show Law-work with conscience as witness.
Hidden things are judged through Messiah Jesus.
Paul turns to the one who rests in Judean identity and Law.
Instruction from Law gives discernment.
The self-image is guide and light.
Knowledge and truth in Law can become teaching identity.
The teacher must become taught.
Commandments expose the teacher’s own inconsistency.
Boasting in Law while transgressing dishonors the Divine.
The Divine name is harmed when identity and action split.
Circumcision profits only as covenant practice.
Uncircumcision doing justice can be counted as circumcision.
Embodied obedience judges letter-without-life.
Outward identity alone is not the fullness.
True covenant identity is inward, heart-circumcision in Spirit, praised by the Divine.

contemplative_note: Romans 2 is the trap door: the judging voice becomes the judged voice. Mystically, it protects the text from being used as domination by turning every accusation into self-examination before Divine impartiality.
